title: Mark-to-Market Accounting
compiled_truth: Enron's aggressive use of mark-to-market accounting, approved by the SEC in 1992, allowed the company to book estimated future profits from long-term contracts as current earnings — inflating reported revenues. Related organizations: [Enron](companies/enron), [SEC](companies/sec), [Arthur Andersen](companies/arthur-andersen).
timeline:
- **1992-01-30** | SEC approves Enron's request to use mark-to-market accounting
- **2000-12-31** | Enron reports $100B in revenues, much mark-to-market
- **2001-10-16** | Enron announces $544M after-tax charge and $1.2B equity reduction
- **2001-11-08** | Enron restates earnings back to 1997, reducing net income by $586M
